$comment: STUB. DO NOT USE AS-IS.
scripts/training/convert_vaf_to_coco17.py refuses to guess a keypoint mapping.
It expects this file to declare, for every COCO-17 keypoint that appears in the
VAF Roboflow export, the integer index it occupies in the VAF kpt_shape array.
How to fill this:
1. Open https://universe.roboflow.com/vaf/bouldering-poses in a browser
(the Roboflow site blocks programmatic fetches with 403, so this step
genuinely requires a human + a browser).
2. Switch the 'Annotations' panel to 'Keypoints' view.
3. Read the keypoint class list top-to-bottom — that is the export order
used in the YOLOv8 Pose export format.
4. For every COCO-17 name below, fill in the matching VAF index.
If a COCO-17 keypoint is NOT annotated by VAF (likely true for the
four head keypoints — left_eye/right_eye/left_ear/right_ear — which
are uncommon in climbing pose datasets), leave the value as null.
The converter will emit those keypoints with visibility=0 in the
YOLO label file, which is the documented behaviour for unannotated
joints.
5. Save a screenshot of the Roboflow keypoint list to
data/manifests/attribution/roboflow-vaf-bouldering-poses-keypoints.png
so the mapping is auditable.
6. Set sourceVerifiedBy / sourceVerifiedAt below to your real name +
ISO date. dataset_sanity will reject this file while either is null.
The stub deliberately leaves every value null so a forgotten edit cannot
silently produce a misaligned model. If the converter is invoked against
this stub, it will refuse with 'unmapped COCO-17 keypoints'.
